title: ricinus communis var. hybridus
intro: Ricinus communis var. hybridus, commonly known as the castor bean plant, is a hybrid variety of the Ricinus communis plant, known for its large, flat seed pods that contain the castor bean, a toxic seed rich in ricin. The castor bean plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been utilized in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of the castor bean plant include the relief of pain and inflammation, improved digestion, and the support of the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Castor oil, extracted from the seeds, is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, salves, and ointments, to promote skin and hair health, and to alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms.
